metadata.title: PC Algorithm: Learning Causal Structure from Data
metadata.note: CLI video: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(manim) · B05 CHANGE · B06 slate · B07 slate · B08 slate · B09 outro.

beats.narration_text: This is Kore for Humanitarians AI.

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · PC Algorithm: Learning Causal Structure from Data

beats.narration_text: Humanitarians AI. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

props.command: PC Algorithm: Learning Causal Structure from Data
props.segment: PC Algorithm: Learning Causal Structure from Data

beats.role_note: the problem, the stakes — BEFORE the CLI
beats.narration_text: You have 5 variables and no theory about which causes which. Can the PC algorithm learn the causal graph from conditional independence tests alone?
beats.visual_intent: title card: 'causal discovery — learning the graph from data'; no domain knowledge assumed

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude. Implement a simplified PC algorithm: build a skeleton via partial correlation tests, orient v-structures, compare to ground truth DAG. Print precision, recall, and structural Hamming distance.

props.command: claude "Implement simplified PC algorithm for causal discovery. Ground truth DAG: X1->X2, X1->X3, X2->X4, X3->X4, X4->X5, X2->X5. Generate n=2000 from linear Gaussian SCM. Implement: skeleton via partial correlation tests (p<0.05), v-structure orientation. Compare to ground truth: print precision, recall, structural Hamming distance."
props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · CAUSAL MACHINE LEARNING — CAUSAL DISCOVERY

beats.narration_text: Read it. The PC algorithm exploits the Markov condition: conditional independencies in data correspond to missing edges in the DAG. It cannot orient all edges — Markov equivalent graphs exist — but it orients v-structures uniquely.

props.title: pc_algorithm.py

beats.narration_text: The animated DAG construction shows the algorithm's steps. The skeleton appears as undirected gray lines. A false positive edge between X1 and X4 appears then disappears when pruned. Then the green arrows orient the true structure. Precision 0.86, recall 1.0, SHD 1.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: animated DAG construction with skeleton, pruning, and orientation steps

beats.narration_text: Now ask Claude to run the PC algorithm at n=200, n=500, n=2000, and n=5000. Show how precision and recall improve with sample size — at what n does the algorithm reliably recover the true skeleton?

props.command:
claude "update pc_algorithm.py:
  -> run at n=200, 500, 2000, 5000 (10 random seeds each)
  -> print mean precision, recall, SHD at each n
  -> identify minimum n for skeleton precision > 0.8"
props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · CAUSAL MACHINE LEARNING — CAUSAL DISCOVERY

beats.narration_text: At n=200, skeleton precision is 0.61 — noisy. At n=500, it crosses 0.75. At n=2000, it reaches 0.86. The PC algorithm needs roughly 500 observations per edge to achieve reliable recovery — a useful rule of thumb for power analysis.
beats.visual_intent: slate: precision/recall/SHD vs n table; convergence curve

beats.narration_text: The PC algorithm trades domain knowledge for data. It learns what domain knowledge usually asserts for free. The cost: it needs data, it struggles with latent variables, and Markov equivalent graphs cannot be distinguished without more assumptions.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: PC algorithm tradeoffs; Markov equivalence class limitation

beats.narration_text: Your move: draw the DAG you currently assume for your domain and run the PC algorithm on your data. Where the algorithm's skeleton contradicts your assumed graph, you have a disagreement between domain knowledge and statistical independence. Investigate both — one of them is wrong.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: run PC algorithm, compare to assumed DAG, investigate disagreements

beats.narration_text: Humanitarians AI. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Humanitarians AI, nikbearbrown dot com.
